summary: axois-utils typosquats axios and runs a postinstall script that beacons host data over HTTP
details: The package name is a one-character edit of the popular HTTP client axios, and ships a postinstall.js that loads child_process and performs outbound HTTP requests (http.request, GET, POST) carrying host identity fields (hostname) collected from the installer's machine. The combination of (a) a typosquat name targeting a top-tier npm package, (b) a lifecycle script that fires on every npm install without user consent, (c) network I/O bound to host-identifying data, and (d) child_process usage in the same install-time script matches the canonical install-time exfiltration / dropper shape. There is no legitimate reason for a thin axios-utility shim to enumerate hostname and beacon it from a postinstall hook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
